quick_skim.title: Zero-Shot Metabolite Prediction from Gene Expression via Physics-Informed Graph Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Predicting metabolite concentrations from gene expression is instrumental for linking regulatory programs to metabolic phenotypes. Prior approaches rely on static enzyme-metabolite mappings and often omit data-driven learning or biochemical constraints, limiting their ability to generalize to new metabolites. We present GAZE (Graph Attention for Zero-shot metabolite Estimation), a physics-informed graph neural network that integrates enzyme expression, Enzyme Commission functional embeddings, and ChemBERTa metabolite descriptors within a unified metabolic graph (5,414 nodes, 16,307 edges). A Metabolite-Conditioned Reader uses each metabolites SMILES embedding to query learned pathway representations, enabling zero-shot prediction with no metabolite-specific parameters. We evaluate GAZE in three scenarios: (i) standard cross-validation on the Cancer Atlas of Metabolic Profiles (18,044 genes, 180 metabolites, 867 cell lines), achieving R2 = 0.816; (ii) leave-one-metabolite-out (LOMO) zero-shot evaluation across 50 held-out metabolites, where the physics-informed variant halves the median R2 deficit relative to a standard GNN baseline (-0.34 vs. -0.72), with 30% of unseen metabolites achieving positive R2; and (iii) external validation on an independent clear cell renal cell carcinoma tissue cohort (220 samples), where GAZE achieves median Spearman{rho} = 0.330 across 214 metabolites without fine-tuning. GAZE outperforms scCellFie, MEBOCOST, and UnitedMet across all evaluation settings.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: provides a method for predicting metabolite concentrations from gene expression, applicable to study metabolic changes in exercise and flap survival
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提供从基因表达预测代谢物浓度的方法，可用于研究运动与皮瓣存活中的代谢变化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提供从基因表达预测代谢物浓度的方法，可用于研究运动与皮瓣存活中的代谢变化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提供从基因表达预测代谢物浓度的方法，可用于研究运动与皮瓣存活中的代谢变化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GAZE uses physics-informed graph neural networks to predict metabolite concentrations from gene expression in a zero-shot manner, enabling metabolic profiling relevant to exercise-induced changes in flap survival.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有方法依赖静态酶-代谢物映射且缺乏泛化能力。该研究提出GAZE，一种物理信息图神经网络，集成酶表达、EC功能嵌入和ChemBERTa代谢物描述符，在代谢图上实现零样本代谢物预测。通过代谢物条件阅读器查询学习到的通路表示，可推广到新代谢物。该方法为研究运动训练导致的皮瓣代谢重塑提供了有力工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有方法依赖静态酶-代谢物映射且缺乏泛化能力。该研究提出GAZE，一种物理信息图神经网络，集成酶表达、EC功能嵌入和ChemBERTa代谢物描述符，在代谢图上实现零样本代谢物预测。通过代谢物条件阅读器查询学习到的通路表示，可推广到新代谢物。该方法为研究运动训练导致的皮瓣代谢重塑提供了有力工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: metabolic changes during exercise and flap survival
